## Discussion

By using a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Place] object as an argument to the [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/AnnotationConstructorOptions], you can, depending on the exact coordinates and other rendering computations, supersede an Apple-provided item on the map. This can reduce potential visual clutter of two icons representing the same place on a single map.

You can also use a `Place` as the first argument when creating a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Annotation], rather than using a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Coordinate] to achieve the same effect.

MapKit JS also supports this capability for the initializer and constructor options for [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/ImageAnnotation] and [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/MarkerAnnotation].

# place

**Interface Property**

An object that allows a custom annotation to potentially supersede a point of interest at the same map coordinates.

## Declaration

```swift
place?: Place | SearchAutocompleteResult;
```

## Discussion

By using a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Place] object as an argument to the [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/AnnotationConstructorOptions], you can, depending on the exact coordinates and other rendering computations, supersede an Apple-provided item on the map. This can reduce potential visual clutter of two icons representing the same place on a single map.

You can also use a `Place` as the first argument when creating a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Annotation], rather than using a [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/Coordinate] to achieve the same effect.

MapKit JS also supports this capability for the initializer and constructor options for [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/ImageAnnotation] and [doc://com.apple.mapkitjs/documentation/MapKitJS/MarkerAnnotation].

## Creating interaction behavior

- **animates**: A Boolean value that determines whether the map animates the annotation.
- **draggable**: A Boolean value that determines whether the user can drag the annotation.
- **enabled**: A Boolean value that determines whether the annotation responds to user interaction.
- **selected**: A Boolean value that determines whether the map displays the annotation in a selected state.
